pattern: Memoized search over the remaining-needs vector tries every special offer that does not overshoot, falling back to buying leftovers at unit price.
hints: The state is the vector of items still needed; from any state you either apply an applicable offer or stop and buy the rest individually.
An offer is applicable only if it does not make any need go negative, since you may not buy more than required.

Recurse on the remaining needs. For each offer that fits, subtract it, add its price, and recurse; also compute the baseline cost of buying every remaining item at unit price. Memoize on the needs tuple and return the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: two offers, one bundles items at a discount
an offer would overshoot a need and must be skipped
single item, single offer, zero needs
offer is more expensive than buying singly
free items via a zero-priced bundle
repeated use of the same offer
no offer helps, pay unit price for all

stress.description: small random store with several offers and modest needs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, 5)
    price = [rng.randrange(0, 11) for _ in range(n)]
    needs = [rng.randrange(0, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    ns = rng.randrange(1, 8)
    special = []
    for _ in range(ns):
        items = [rng.randrange(0, 4) for _ in range(n)]
        if all(v == 0 for v in items):
            items[rng.randrange(0, n)] = 1
        offer = items + [rng.randrange(0, 30)]
        special.append(offer)
    return [price, special, needs]
stress.note: small needs keep the exponential search fast; every offer has a non-zero item
